Evangelos Papoutsellis
home about research dissemination

Henry Moseley X-ray Imaging Facility, School of Materials

University of Manchester

3.309, Alan Turing building Manchester, M13 9PY


evangelos.papoutsellis@manchester.ac.uk epapoutsellis@gmail.com

Hello, my name is Evangelos (or better Vaggelis) Papoutsellis and I am a Research Associate at the University of Manchester. I work in the Collaborative Computational Project in Tomographic Imaging, aka CCPi headed by Profs Philip Withers and William Lionheart.

During January 2016 - October 2018, I was a Research Associate at the Institut Denis Poisson, Université d’Orléans and at the Faculté de Médecine, Université de Tours. I was working with Prof. Maïtine Bergounioux and Prof. Clovis Tauber in dynamic PET reconstruction and with Prof. Christophe Destrieux in validating in & ex vivo MRI brain registration.

Prior to that I completed my PhD under the supervision of Prof. Carola-Bibiane Schönlieb at the Cambridge Image Analysis group of Department of Applied Mathematics and Theoretical Physics at the University of Cambridge.

news

Jan, 2020 Collaborative Computational Project in Tomographic Imaging was renewed until April 2025
Dec, 2019 Hackathon: Core Imaging Library, 16-18 December 2019
Nov, 2019 Organising Training cource in Synergistic Reconstruction Symposium with Evelina Ametova and Jakob Jørgensen for the Core Imaging Library of CCPi.
